obligations: Single restaurant at Selected Site only (Section 2(a))
No relocation without written consent; relocation fee $12,500 + Lost Royalty Fee (Section 2(c))
Designated Area determined by franchisor (Section 2(d))
Initial term: 10 years (Section 3(a))
Four 5-year renewal terms (Section 3(b))
Renewal requires then-current FA form (may be materially different)
Initial franchise fee: $50,000 (Section 4(a))
Royalty: 5% of Restaurant Revenue, monthly by 15th (Section 4(b))
Brand Fund Contribution: 0.5% of Restaurant Revenue (Section 4(c))
EFT authorization required for all payments (Section 4(f))
Interest on late payments: lesser of 2%/month or max legal rate (Section 4(h))
Submit Site Report within 120 days of FA effective date (Section 6(a))
Site Evaluation Fee: $1,000 per site; max 5 evaluations (Section 6(a))
Must use approved architect (Section 6(e))
May select own general contractor subject to approval (Section 6(e))
Must submit proposed lease for consent (Section 6(c))
Must open by Opening Deadline per Exhibit C (Section 6(f))
Pre-Opening Royalty: $3,000/week if past deadline (Section 6(f))
Termination if not opened within 1 year of deadline despite paying Pre-Opening Royalty
Designated Principal, Operations Leader, GM, 3+ Managers must complete Approved Management Training (Section 5(a))
Taking Over Certificate required before training (Section 5(a))
Training must complete 60 days before opening (Section 5(a))
Opening Crew: franchisee reimburses $10K-$20K (Section 5(b))
Continuing training every 6 months; annual operations conference mandatory (Section 5(d)-(e))
On-site remedial training at franchisor's then-current rate (Section 5(f))
Failure to complete training = prohibition on opening or FA termination (Section 5)
Must comply with Manuals (626 pages) and all Standards (Section 10(a))
Must maintain Restaurant appearance to franchisor standards (Section 10(f))
Must use only approved menu items, products, services (Section 10(e))
Must participate in mystery shopper program ($150/visit) (Section 10(l))
Must participate in food safety inspections (Steritech, $262/quarter) (Section 10(m))
Must permit inspections and audits at any time (Section 10(k)-(o))
Franchisor has unlimited right to access POS data (Section 7)

Non-curable immediate termination: misrepresentation, failure to open, abandonment, felony, unauthorized transfer, unauthorized disclosure, pattern defaults (3 in 12 months or 10 in term) (Section 20(a)(1))
Automatic termination: bankruptcy, assignment for creditors, receiver appointment (Section 20(a)(1))
Curable defaults (30 days): payment failure, inaccurate reporting, law violations, Brand Audit failure, inspection deficiencies, lease non-compliance (Section 20(a)(2))
Event of Default Fee: $12,500 per event (Section 20(b))
Pre-Opening Royalty in lieu of termination: $3,000/week (Section 20(c))
Cross-default with ADA and other FAs (Section 20(a)(1))
Pay all amounts within 15 days (Section 21(a))
Pay liquidated damages within 30 days: min $500,000 or royalty-based formula (Section 21(a))
Cease operations immediately (Section 21(b))
Discontinue all Marks, System, Confidential Information (Section 21(b))
Return Manuals (Section 21(b))
De-identify Restaurant (Section 21(c))
Assign/cancel phone, domain, email, social media (Section 21(d))
Comply with noncompete for 2 years (Section 21)
No assignment by franchisor restricted (Section 18(a))
All transfers require franchisor approval, not unreasonably withheld (Section 18(b))
Transfer conditions: full compliance, qualified transferee, then-current FA, $12,500 fee, general release, no ROFR exercise (Section 18(c))
Right of first refusal: franchisor can match any offer (Section 18(f))
Death/disability: 6 months to transfer to approved party (Section 18(e))
Non-controlling interest transfer: 15 days notice, no Competitive Business involvement, no felony (Section 18(h))
Security interest: may grant but secured party cannot take possession or operate Restaurant (Section 18(i))
During term: no Competitive Business in the U.S. (Section 19(a))
Post-term: 2 years within (1) Designated Area, (2) 3 miles of Restaurant, (3) 3 miles of any other Restaurant (Section 19(a))
Competitive Business definition: (1) same/substantially similar to Fish & Chips including >=25% British fish and chips, OR (2) any restaurant identified by/promoted with celebrity chef (Section 1(o))
Applicable to franchisee, affiliates, and all principals
Personal and unconditional guaranty from all principals (Exhibit A)
Jointly and severally liable for all FA obligations including payment
Spouses NOT required to sign
Bound by confidentiality and noncompete provisions
Scenario 1 (<3 years remaining): greater of (avg royalty x months remaining) or $500,000
Scenario 2 (>=3 years, operated >=2 years): avg royalty x 24 months
Scenario 3 (>=3 years, operated <2 years): system avg royalty x 24 months
Described as 'reasonable, bona fide pre-estimate of damages and not a penalty'
Step 1: Non-binding mediation (AAA, Dallas TX) (Section 26(b))
Step 2: Binding arbitration (AAA, Dallas TX) (Section 26(c))
Step 3: Litigation in Dallas TX courts (Section 26(e))
Governing law: Texas (Section 27)
Subject to applicable state law
Waiver of jury trial
Waiver of punitive damages (except for certain claims)
Commercial general liability: $1M per occurrence / $2M aggregate
Liquor liability: $1M per occurrence
All Risk property insurance: replacement value
Building and personal property insurance
Builders All Risk and vacant dwelling liability
Crime insurance: $10K inside, $10K outside, $25K employee dishonesty
Auto liability: $1M
Employment practices liability: $1M
Workers' comp per state law
Umbrella: $5M over CGL, liquor, auto, products, employers' liability
Insurer must have A.M. Best A- (Excellent) or better, Financial Size X or higher
Franchisor and affiliates named as additional insureds
30 days written notice of modification/cancellation
Franchisee must indemnify, defend, and hold harmless franchisor and affiliates
Covers all claims arising from franchise operation
Includes attorneys' fees and costs
Survives termination/expiration
Neither party liable for Force Majeure Events
5-day notice requirement
If suspension exceeds 90 days and would constitute default, franchisor may terminate
Inability to pay fees is NOT a Force Majeure Event
Upon expiration or termination, franchisor may purchase assets and/or assume lease
60-day exercise window from termination/expiration
Purchase price determined by fair market value provisions in Section 22
